segments.s430-g25: کزان is a compound prepositional/relative particle meaning 'from that' or 'of that'. خستگی is the noun 'fatigue/weariness' serving as the subject of the clause. بیم جانست is a terse predicative phrase: بیم ('danger/risk') + جانست (literally 'is-of-life' here functioning to mean 'concerns life' or 'is life-threatening'); و بس is a short conjunction + adverb meaning 'and (that) is enough'.
segments.s430-g26: بران گونه = 'in/of that manner' (بران = 'in/thereon/that', گونه = 'manner/type'). خسته is the adjective 'exhausted/tired' describing an implied person or thing. ندیدست is a perfective/verblike parenthetical meaning 'has not been seen' (i.e. 'no one has seen'), and کس here means 'anyone/no one' (in negative contexts it yields 'no one'). The clause states that nobody has been seen in that exhausted state.
segments.s430-g27: همان رخش = 'that very Rakhsh' (همان = 'that/the same', رخش = the horse's name/‘steed’). گویی here is used parenthetically as a comment 'one might say / it seems' (the provided lemma senses did not include this evidential/quotative use, so -1 is given). که introduces the following subordinate clause; بیجان شدست is a compound of the adjective بیجان 'lifeless' plus شدست 'has become', giving 'has become lifeless'.
segments.s430-g28: ز پیکان = 'from (the) arrows' (ز = 'from/because of', پیکان = 'arrows'). تنِ ش is 'his body' (تن = 'body', ش = enclitic 'his'). زار و بیجان شدست is a coordinated predicate: زار 'wretched/miserable' and بیجان 'lifeless' with شدست 'has become', describing the condition of his body as a result of the arrows.
segments.s430-g29: بیامد is the past-tense verb 'came'. برین کشور is a prepositional phrase meaning 'into/onto that country' (بر here functions 'toward/to', the element -ین is the pronoun/suffix marking 'that' in برین, and کشور = 'country/realm'). اسفندیار is the subject proper noun.
segments.s430-g30: نکوبد is a literary/historic third-person verb meaning 'he strikes/struck' (here used as a habitual or characteristic action). همی is an archaic/progressive particle marking continuous/habitual aspect. جز در کارزار = 'except in battle' (جز = 'except', در = 'in', کارزار = 'battle/ battlefield'), so the clause means he strikes only in combat.
segments.s430-g31: نجوید is a literary negative/present form meaning 'he does not seek' or 'he did not desire'. همی marks habitual/continuous aspect. کشور و تاج و تخت is a list 'the realm and the crown and the throne' (objects of نجوید): کشور = 'country/realm', تاج = 'crown', تخت = 'throne'.
segments.s430-g32: This clause is compressed/literary: برو is ambiguous in isolation (dictionary senses given are 'go / be off') but here functions idiomatically with بار and خواهد to mean taking/bearing a load — the provided senses do not match that exact meaning, so I mark it -1. بار here means 'burden' (بار = 'burden/responsibility'), خواهد is the auxiliary/future 'he will', همی again a continuous/habitual marker, با = 'with', درخت = 'tree'. Together the clause says roughly 'he will carry/bear the burden with a tree' (a terse, poetic way to say he will shoulder a heavy burden even with a tree).
segments.s430-g33: بدو is an adverbial/indirect-object phrase 'to him' placed before the verb for emphasis; گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (here the listed senses did not include the verbal sense); سیمرغ is the subject (the speaker) following the verb, a common poetic word order (V S). کای پهلوان is a vocative ('O champion') addressing the addressee.
segments.s430-g34: مباش is the negative imperative 'do not be / do not become' addressed to the vocative just given. اندرین کار is a prepositional-nominal phrase 'in this matter' (اندرین = 'in this', کار = 'affair/task') and خسته‌روان ('the weary ones' / 'weary‑souls') qualifies or characterizes that affair — together: 'in this work of the weary'. The whole phrase gives the content of the prohibition.
segments.s430-g35: سزدگر here functions as a predicate/adverbial 'if it is fitting' or 'as is fitting'; نمایی is a hortative/subjunctive/optative form 'show (may you show)' or 'do show' (the -ی ending is compatible with jussive/subjunctive in classical usage). به من marks the indirect object 'to me', and رخش را is the definite direct object ('your/the Rakhsh') with را as the object marker. Word order is typical poetic order: qualifying adverb/predicate + verb + indirect object + direct object.
segments.s430-g36: همان is a demonstrative 'that very / the same'; سرفراز is an adjectival epithet 'proud/victorious'; جهان‌بخش is a compound epithet 'world‑bestowing' (an honorific); را again is the object marker repeating/emphasizing the definite horse. The segment is an appositive description specifying which horse is meant.
segments.s430-g37: This is an SOV-style clause with the subject name 'زال' placed after the verb for narrative emphasis: کسی ('someone') is the (indefinite) object, سوی رستم 'toward Rostam' is the directional phrase, فرستاد is the past-tense verb 'sent', and زال is the agent (subject) following the verb.
segments.s430-g38: که introduces a purpose/result clause here (sense 'so that / in order that'). لختی is a temporal adverb 'for a short while'; به چاره = 'for/with a remedy' ('به' marking purpose/aim and چاره = 'remedy/solution'); برافراز is a jussive/subjunctive/infinitival form 'may raise / to raise' and یال is the thing raised 'the mane'. Together they state the intended purpose of the sending.
segments.s430-g39: بفرمای here is reported speech/command ('he said/ordered' or a polite imperative 'command that...') used to introduce what is to be done. تا functions as a purposive marker 'so that / in order that', and رخش را همچنان is the definite object phrase 'Rakhsh (the horse), in the same way' with را marking the object and همچنان meaning 'as before / likewise'.
segments.s430-g40: بیارند is a 3rd-person plural present/subjunctive jussive 'bring (them)' or 'may they bring' (used after تا for purpose/command) — the provided senses did not include the verbal sense. پیش من is a prepositional phrase 'before me / in my presence', اندر زمان is a temporal adverbial 'at that time' or 'immediately/then'; the order places the verb phrase before its adverbials as often in poetry.
segments.s430-g73: چرا is the question word 'why' at sentence-initial position. رزم جستی is a compact predicate: رزم (battle) + جستی (past 2nd-person of جستن used here in the sense 'to take up/seek' a fight), so literally 'you sought battle' = 'you engaged in battle'. The preposition-like ز before the name marks the adversary/target ('against' or 'with') in poetic usage: 'against Esfandiyar'.
segments.s430-g74: که introduces a relative clause describing اسفندیار ('who' / 'for he'). او هست is the copula construction 'he is' (present/persistent state). رویین‌تن is an archaic/epic epithet meaning 'iron-bodied' or 'armored', followed by و نامدار 'and renowned' — standard adjective sequence following the copula.
segments.s430-g75: بدو (to him) is an indirect-object clitic/phrase placed before the verb. گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (here functioning as 'said to him'); رستم‌گر is the nominative/agent ('the Rostam-like man' or 'Rostam‑man') and او را 'him' is the object marked by را. ز بند ('from/of bondage') specifies relation to the object — 'concerning him/from his bonds.'
segments.s430-g76: نبودی is an archaic/poetic past subjunctive/conditional form 'were not' / 'had not been' (negated بودن). It sets up a conditional clause that is elliptical (if he had not been ...). دل من نگشتی نژند is the result clause: دل من 'my heart' (subject/possessive), نگشتی (past/become) = 'became', and نژند is a predicative adjective 'base/ignoble'.
segments.s430-g77: مرا کشتن is a verbal-noun construction where مرا is the object of the infinitive کشتن ('to kill me'). کشتن (verbal noun/infinitive) functions as the subject of the impersonal predicate آسان‌تر آید ('comes easier' = 'is easier'). ز ننگ marks the standard of comparison: 'than disgrace' or 'than shame'. Note a poetic clitic split: the object marker را may appear separated (here shown as separate tokens).
segments.s430-g78: وگر introduces the counterfactual/alternating condition 'but if' / 'and if'. بازمانم is first-person singular present/subjunctive (or optative) of بازماندن 'I remain / survive' used in the protasis. به جایی 'to some place' indicates movement or withdrawal, and ز جنگ 'from the war' specifies the domain being left — 'away from the battle.'
segments.s430-g79: چنین داد پاسخ is a fixed epic phrasing: چنین 'thus' + داد (past of دادن, 'gave') + پاسخ ('answer') — 'thus gave an answer'. کز is a contracted poetic form (که + ز) meaning 'which/from' and introduces the source: 'which was from Esfandiyar' (i.e., 'Esfandiyar answered').
segments.s430-g80: اگر marks a conditional 'if'. سر بجا آوری is a verbal phrase: سر 'head' as object and بجا آوری (بجا + آوری) = 'bring (back) in place / bring back (intact)' — here 'bring the head (back)'. نیست عار is an inverted verbless copular clause common in classical Persian: نیست 'is not' + عار 'shame' = 'there is no shame / it is not disgraceful.'
segments.s430-g9: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'. پاسی ازان تیره شب is a noun phrase meaning 'a portion/part of that dark night' (پاسی + ازان + تیره شب). درگذشت is a past verb 'passed'/'elapsed' used here for time; the verb applies to the whole temporal phrase ('when a part of that night passed').
segments.s430-g10: This main clause has the subject 'تو' ('you') and the past verb گفتی ('you said') followed by a reported clause introduced by چو as a simile ('like/as'). آهن سیاه ابر گشت is a verbless simile structure where گشت (past of گشتن) means 'became/turned', so 'the cloud became black as iron.'
segments.s430-g11: همانگه is an adverb meaning 'at that moment'. چو introduces a subordinate temporal clause 'when' (here 'when the bird...'). مرغ از هوا is 'the bird from the air' (i.e., looking down from the sky). بنگرید is an older past-form of نگاه کردن/نگریستن 'looked'/'gazed'.
segments.s430-g12: درخشیدن is used as a noun/gerund 'the shining' or 'gleam'. آتش تیز is a noun + adjective 'fire (or a flame) sharp/keen'—here 'a sharp/keen fire' or 'the fire's keen gleam'. دید is the past verb 'saw' (the bird as subject is implied from the previous clause).
segments.s430-g13: نشسته is a predicative/participial adjective 'sitting' or 'seated'. برش here is attached to the verb phrase and would normally indicate the thing he sat on (however the provided sense list doesn't include the locative meaning). زال is the proper name 'Zāl'. با درد و غم is a prepositional phrase 'with pain and sorrow' describing his emotional/physical state.
segments.s430-g14: ز (poetic form of از) marks cause or source 'because of/from'. پرواز مرغ is a genitive phrase 'the bird's flight'. اندر آمد is a compound verb meaning 'came in / became' (here 'became'), and دژم is a predicate adjective 'sullen/gloomy' describing Zāl's resulting mood.
segments.s430-g15: بشد (classical) here functions as a past/preterite 'he moved/advanced' or 'it happened that he came'; پیش means 'before / in front of' (i.e., toward Zāl). با + عود زال is an instrumental phrase 'with Zāl's ʿūd' — 'عود' can mean the lute (instrument) or agarwood; context suggests the instrument. از فراز is a locative/source phrase 'from on high' or 'from above.'
segments.s430-g16: ستودش is the past tense 'praised him' (ستود + ضمیرش). فراوان here modifies the verb as 'abundantly' or 'much'. و is the coordinator 'and'. بردش in this context is verbal and means 'took/carried away' or 'led' (but the supplied senses don't include the verbal 'take'); نماز is 'prayer' or a ritual prayer. Together بردش نماز likely means 'he took/received his prayer' or 'he led him in prayer.'
segments.s430-g17: به پیشش is a prepositional phrase meaning 'before him / in his presence' (به + پیشش). سه مجمر پر از بوی is a noun phrase 'three censers full of scent' where پر از takes the following noun with از marking the content ('full of ...'). کرد is the past of کردن (here 'placed/made') placed at the end in typical classical Persian word order; the sentence omits an explicit subject (understood from context).
segments.s430-g18: ز خون جگر marks the source with the poetic/archaic z (ز) 'from' + 'blood of the jigar' (here idiomatically the seat of feeling, i.e. 'the heart'). بر دو رخ 'upon both cheeks' (دو modifies رخ). جوی کرد literally 'made a stream' (جوی = stream; کرد = made/did), i.e. caused a stream to run down the cheeks — a compact poetic expression where object and adverbial precede the verb.
segments.s430-g19: بدو گفت is the common formula 'he said to him' (بدو = to him; گفت = said). سیمرغ is the speaker (the mythic bird). شاها is a vocative 'O king' inserted before the content of the question. چه بود is the interrogative 'what was (it)' with چه + past auxiliary بود.
segments.s430-g20: که introduces the subordinate clause 'that/which' or effectively 'that caused...'; آمد ... به دود uses آمد with به + دود to mean 'turned/rose into smoke' or 'came as smoke.' ازین سان = 'in this manner' (از + این/ین + سان). نیازت is مستعار / suffixed object 'your supplication' (نیاز + -ت). Word order places the possessed noun and adverbial before the verb, a typical poetic order.
segments.s430-g21: چنین گفت = 'thus he said' (introducing reported speech). کاین = 'that this' (demonstrative + enclitic k-), followed by بد 'this evil' and the indirect object به دشمن 'to the enemy.' رساد is an archaic/jussive form meaning 'may it reach / let it come to' (optative/subjunctive sense), so the clause expresses a wish that the evil befall the enemy.
segments.s430-g22: که here gives the reason 'for/because.' بر من رسید = 'fell upon me / reached me' (رسید past 'reached, came upon'). از بد بدنژاد = 'from an ignoble-born (person)' where از marks source/agent and بدنژاد is a noun/adjective 'low-born, ignoble.' The clause explains that the speaker suffered because of that low-born person.
segments.s430-g23: تن رستم = 'the body of Rostam' (noun + name), with شیردل ('lion‑hearted') as an epithet in apposition. خسته شد is a two-word predicate 'became tired' (خسته + شد), a normal past-tense description of physical exhaustion.
segments.s430-g24: ازان خستگی = 'from that fatigue' (ازان = from that). جان من بسته شد literally 'my life was closed/tied' (بسته شد = 'became closed'), a poetic way to say the speaker's life/strength was shut off or nearly lost. The possessive من is attached to جان and the predicate follows in typical Persian order.
segments.s430-g161: تنِ زال را is a definite object phrase (تن = “body”, modified by the name زال), with را marking it as the direct object. مرغ is the subject placed after the object (poetic inversion), پدرود is a noun meaning “farewell” used with کرد to form a verbal phrase, and کرد is the simple past auxiliary meaning “did/made” — so مرغ پدرود کرد = “the bird bade farewell.”
segments.s430-g162: ازو is an older/poetic form of از او = “from him/it.” تار here means the weaving-warp (تار). وز should be read as the literary contraction و + ز (i.e. “and from”), a poetic linker (not one of the dictionary senses listed), خویشتن = “itself,” پود = the weft, and کرد is the past verb “made/took.” The clause means literally “from him the warp, and from itself the weft it made/took.”
segments.s430-g163: ازان جایگه is an archaic prepositional phrase (ازان = از آن) meaning “from that place.” نیک‌دل (an epithet functioning as the subject) precedes the past verb برپرید (برپرید = “leapt up / took flight”), so the clause reads “from that place the kind‑hearted one leapt up.”
segments.s430-g164: چو here means “when.” اندر هوا is the locative phrase “in the air”; رستم is the subject and او را (او + object marker را) is the object “him.” بدید is the simple past of دیدن = “saw.” Word order is subject (رستم) + object (او را) + verb (بدید), giving “when Rostam saw him in the air.”
segments.s430-g165: یکی here means “someone / a certain person.” آتش چوب is a poetic compound “fire‑wood” or “burning stick,” with چوب specifying the wooden item; پرتاب is the noun/verb “throw,” and کرد is the past auxiliary “did/threw.” The clause = “someone threw a burning stick / firewood.”
segments.s430-g166: دلش is دل + enclitic ش = “his heart.” The object marker را follows (دلش را). بران is a contraction of بر آن = “for/on that,” modifying رزم (“battle”). شاداب is an adjective meaning “vigorous/eager,” and کرد is the past verb “made.” The clause literally: “it made his heart vigorous for that battle.”
segments.s430-g167: یکی = “someone.” تیز پیکان = “a sharp/speedy arrow.” بدو (به او) = “to/into him” (classical clitic), در = “in,” and نشاند is an archaic past meaning “caused to be set/placed/embedded” (here used of an arrow being fixed in a body). Word order places object phrases before the verb, typical in older/poetic Persian.
segments.s430-g168: چپ و راست = “left and right.” پرها is the plural “the feathers,” بروبر is a literary/adverbial word meaning “face‑to‑face / exactly opposite / evenly,” and نشاند (same verb as above) = “placed / fixed.” The clause describes feathers being set on both left and right sides exactly opposite one another.
segments.s430-g121: برفتند (past plural) = 'they departed'; و simply coordinates clauses. ما را uses ما with the object marker را (direct object) so the phrase ما را سپردند means 'they entrusted/handed us (the thing)'. سپردند is past plural 'they entrusted/handed over'. جای here is the object 'place/position' that was handed over; its pragmatic object relation is signaled by را earlier.
segments.s430-g122: جهان را: جهان marked by را (here topical/definite object) = 'the world' (i.e. the realm). چنین است is a fixed verbless/nominal comment pattern 'such is ...' (چنین = 'such'); است is the copula 'is'. آیین و رای are two coordinated nouns ('custom/practice' and 'counsel/opinion') serving as the predicate 'such is the world's custom and counsel.'
segments.s430-g123: همی راند uses the archaic/poetic prefix همی to mark continuous or habitual aspect — 'kept riding/was riding'. راند is past of راندن 'to ride/drive'. تا ... رسید sets an extent clause: تا (until) + پیش دریا (in front of/the sea) + رسید 'he reached/arrived', so together 'he rode on until he reached the sea ahead.'
segments.s430-g124: ز here is the classical/genitive 'of/from' linking سیمرغ to the following description; روی هوا is a locative phrase 'on the air/in the sky' where روی functions as 'on/upon' (modern 'رویِ هوا' = 'on the air/sky'). تیره is an adjective 'dark/ominous' modifying an implied face/form, and دید is the past verb 'he saw'. Together: 'he saw the Simurgh's dark form in the air.'
segments.s430-g125: چو آمد = 'when it came' (چو = 'when'). به نزدیک دریا = 'to/near the sea' (به + نزدیک + noun). فراز here is an adverbial 'on high/from above' that syntactically links to the following verb فرود آمد; in the poet's line it is placed at the end of this segment but modifies the descent that follows.
segments.s430-g126: فرود آمد is a compound past tense 'descended / alighted' (فرود as noun/verb 'descent' used in a verbal compound with آمد). آن مرغ = 'that bird'; گردنفراز is an adjectival epithet 'neck‑raising / proud‑necked' describing the bird. The whole clause: 'that proud-necked bird descended.'
segments.s430-g127: به رستم: indirect object/dative 'to Rostam'. نمود here is the past singular of نمودن used impersonally/ergatively 'appeared / was shown' (i.e. 'appeared to him'). آن زمان 'that time/then' sets the temporal frame. راه خشک = 'the dry path/road' is the subject that 'appeared'. Word order is topical: indirect object + verb + time + subject.
segments.s430-g128: همی آمد again marks continuous action 'kept coming'. از باد او is a genitive/ablative phrase 'from its wind/breeze' (او = 'it' referring to the bird). بوی مشک = 'the scent of musk' (بوی = smell/scent, مشک = musk). The clause means 'from its breeze the scent of musk kept coming.'
segments.s430-g41: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. The clause has the proper name رستم as subject, followed by the locative/adverbial phrase بران تند بالا ('on/there, swift and high') and the past tense verb رسید ('reached'). بران is an older contraction of بر آن ('on/that'), and تند here functions adverbially/attributively with بالا to describe the manner/direction of reaching.
segments.s430-g42: همان is a determiner 'that/the same' emphasising identity; مرغ روشن‌دل is the noun with a compound adjective (روشن‌دل, 'bright‑hearted') after it; او را is the object pronoun with the enclitic object marker را, and بدید is the simple past 'saw' (3rd singular). Note the classical prefixed past form (بـ‑) on the verb.
segments.s430-g43: بدو گفت here is a two‑word sequence meaning 'to him it said' (بدو = 'to him/unto it' as indirect object); گفت is the past 'said' (classical usage). کای is a vocative 'O', followed by the compound epithet ژنده پیل بلند ('ragged/tatter'd elephant, tall'), a poetic insult or ironic epithet addressed to the addressee.
segments.s430-g44: ز دست introduces agency/means 'by/through (your) hand' (ز = 'by/from', دست here = 'hand/power'), که begins a subordinate clause 'that/which' and governs گشتی, an archaic/past 2nd‑singular form meaning 'you became' or 'you turned (into)'. بدین سان is the fixed phrase 'in this manner/so', with ب‑ the classical prefix and سان 'so/thus', and نژند is a predicate adjective 'base/worthless'.
segments.s430-g45: چرا is the interrogative 'why'. رزم جستی is a verbal phrase 'you sought/undertook battle' (رزم = battle, جستی = 2nd singular past of 'to seek/undertake'), and ز before اسفندیار marks the adversary/target 'with/regarding Esfandiyār' in classical usage.
segments.s430-g46: The second چرا repeats the rhetorical question. آتش افگندی is an idiom 'you kindled/set fire' (آتش = fire; افگندی = 2nd singular past of افکندن, here 'to kindle/throw fire'), and اندر کنار means 'at/on the edge/bank' (اندر = 'in/at', کنار = 'edge/side'). The whole line asks why he provoked destructive action there.
segments.s430-g47: بدو گفت again 'to him it said' (بدو = 'to him'); گفت is the past 'said'. زال‌ای is a vocative 'O Zal', and خداوند مهر is an honorific epithet 'lord/master of affection' (خداوند used as a polite/honorific title here, followed by مهر 'love/affection').
segments.s430-g48: چو here means 'as/when'. اکنون 'now' with نمودی (2nd singular past of نمودن) 'you have shown' introduces what follows; بما is dative 'to us', and پاک چهر is an adjectival phrase 'pure-faced' (پاک = pure, چهر = face/appearance) describing what has been shown to the speakers.
segments.s430-g1: ببودند is an archaic/past plural verb meaning 'they were' (narrative past). هر دو = 'both' (هر here = 'each/every', paired with دو). بران رای مند is an adjectival phrase 'possessing that opinion' (بران = 'on/that', رای = 'opinion', مند = suffix '‑ful/possessing'); the phrase follows the subject due to poetic inversion, so the full meaning is 'they both were of that opinion.'
segments.s430-g2: سپهبد is the subject ('the spahbed'). برآمد is past 'arose/rose up'. به بالـا بلند is a directional/adverbial phrase: به = 'toward/to', بالا = 'up/high', بلند = 'lofty/high' — doubled adjectives for poetic emphasis, so 'rose up to a high place.' Word order is subject + verb + directional phrase.
segments.s430-g3: از ایوان sets the origin/location 'from the iwan' (از = 'from'). سه مجمر = 'three braziers' (numeral before noun). پر آتش is a postpositive description 'full of fire' (پر = 'full (of)', آتش = 'fire'). ببرد here functions as a narrative past meaning 'he/they took/carried' (classical poetic form).
segments.s430-g4: برفتند is past plural 'they departed/went off'. با او = 'with him'. سه هشیار = 'three alert/watchful (men)' (hِشیار used as an apposed noun). و گرد — گرد here means the accompanying retinue/troop (archaic epic usage). The verb at the start governs the following prepositional phrase and apposition.
segments.s430-g5: فسونگر is the subject 'the sorcerer'. چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'. بر تیغ بالا رسید = 'reached up to the sword' (بر = 'to/toward', تیغ = 'sword', بالا = 'up/above', رسید = past 'reached'). The verb arrives at the end as typical in Persian subordinate clauses.
segments.s430-g6: ز دیبا = 'from the brocade' (ز = poetic 'from'). یکی پر = 'one feather' (یکی = 'one', پر = 'feather') as the direct object. بیرون کشید is a verbal complex 'pulled out' (بیرون = 'out', کشید = past 'pulled'); the verb comes at the end, giving 'he pulled out one feather from the brocade.'
segments.s430-g7: ز مجمر = 'from the brazier' (z = poetic 'from'). یکی = 'one' (here 'one of them' or 'one [brazier/fire]'). آتشی is a noun/phrase meaning 'a fire' or 'a blaze' (آتشی used as 'a fiery thing'). برفروخت is a poetic past 'kindled/inflamed' (transitive), so 'he kindled one blaze from the brazier.'
segments.s430-g8: به بالای آن = 'above it / on its top' (به + بالای = 'to the top of'). پر = 'a feather' (subject). لختی is an adverb 'for a short while' (not 'nakedness' here). بسوخت is past 'burned/was burned' (intransitive), so the clause means 'a feather above it burned for a short while.' Word order places the locative phrase first, then subject + adverb + verb.
segments.s430-g97: بدو (be-do) is a prepositional phrase meaning 'to him' (he said to him) placed before the verb — a common poetic word order. گفت here is the verb 'he said' (past). کز introduces a subordinate clause meaning 'that / because', and the second گفت is a noun meaning 'word' or 'saying' (’your saying’). تو is the 2nd-person clitic 'your/you' attached to the noun, and نَگذرَم (negated present/subjunctive) literally 'I do not let pass / I do not forgive' — the negative prefix ن- plus گذرَم expresses 'I will not let it pass/forgive', linking the clause as the content of what was said.
segments.s430-g98: وگر is a conditional 'and if / but if'. تیغ is 'sword' (object). بارد is a poetic/archaic present meaning 'falls' or 'rains' (from باریدن) used here as a verb — 'a sword should fall/rain'. هوا means 'the air/sky', and بر سرم is the locative phrase 'upon my head' (بر = on/upon, سرم = my head). Together the phrase expresses a concessive condition ('even if swords rain from the sky on my head').
segments.s430-g99: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so' that prefaces reported speech. گفت is the verb 'said' (past) and سیمرغ is the subject 'the Simurgh'. کز introduces the following genitive/ablative phrase 'from / by the way of', راه مهر = 'the path/way of مهر' where مهر here means 'love/affection' (a poetic use). The clause sets the manner/source ('from the way of love').
segments.s430-g100: بگویم is a first-person present/subjunctive used to express 'I will tell' or 'let me tell' (classical Persian often uses ب- plus subjunctive for intention). کنون = 'now'. با تو (written باتو) = 'to you / with you' (here 'to you'). راز = 'secret' and سپهر = 'the sky/heavens'. Word order is Verb–adverb–object phrase; the verb introduces the direct speech act directed at the addressee.
segments.s430-g101: که here begins a content/relative clause ('that / for'), and هرکس که is the indefinite conditional 'whoever'. او refers back to the third person (here Esfandiyar or the person named), and خونِ اسفندیار is the object 'the blood of Esfandiyar' (خون in construct with the name). The clause sets up the general condition: 'whoever causes Esfandiyar's blood to be shed...'.
segments.s430-g102: بریزد is a present/subjunctive form meaning 'sheds/pours' (used here for 'whoever sheds'); ورا = 'on him/thereupon' (referring to that person). بشکرد is an archaic/poetic form meaning 'will break/shatter' (بشکند in modern Persian) and روزگار here is 'fate' or 'fortune'. The phrase literally says 'fate will break him' — an idiom meaning fate will ruin or punish him.
segments.s430-g103: همان نیز = 'likewise / the same (also)', referring back to the previous warning. تا زنده باشد is a temporal clause 'as long as he is alive' where تا = 'until/as long as' and باشد is the subjunctive/present 'be/remain' used in a temporal subordinate clause. ز = poetic contraction of از 'from', and رنج = 'suffering' — together 'from suffering'.
segments.s430-g104: رهایی نیابد: رهایی = 'release/freedom', نیابد is a negative present/subjunctive of یافتن 'to find/attain' meaning 'does not/ will not obtain' (i.e. 'will not find release'). نماندش is نماند + -ش (object clitic) meaning 'does not remain for him / will not be left to him', and گنج = 'treasure' (literal or figurative wealth). The order places the predicate before the affected noun, a poetic inversion: 'no treasure remains for him.'
segments.s430-g65: "بران" is a temporal/conjunctive adverb meaning 'thereupon' or 'on that (occasion)'; it sets the scene. "همنشان رخش را پیش خواست" literally places the object (رخش with object marker را) after the descriptor همنشان ('his matching/fellow') and uses خواست (past of خواستن) to mean 'called/wanted/beckoned forward' — پیش here carries the sense 'forward/before'. Word order is subject omitted/understood; the verb خواست governs the object رخش را.
segments.s430-g66: فرو کرد is a compound where فرو (down) + کرد (past 'did/made') expresses a downward thrust or plunging action. منقار is the noun 'beak' and بر + دست راست marks the surface/target ('upon the right hand') — دست راست is a postpositive noun phrase 'right hand'. The subject is understood from context; کرد is simple past.
segments.s430-g67: برون کرد is 'drew out/removed' (برون 'out/forth' + کرد past 'did'), followed by the object پیکان شش 'six arrows' (number after noun, a poetic order). از گردنش marks origin 'from his neck' (گردن + enclitic ـش 'his').
segments.s430-g68: نبد here is an archaic/poetic negative copula (≃ نبود) meaning 'there was not'. The clause that follows describes a condition of his body: خسته‌گر (an adjective 'tiring, wearisome') + بسته (passive/adjectival 'bound/tied') + جایی 'any place/spot' + تنش 'his body' — overall: 'there was not a place on his body that was not bound/tied by the tiring (band)'. Word order is compacted and somewhat archaic, so modifiers precede the noun cluster.
segments.s430-g69: همانگه is a temporal adverb 'at that moment'. خروشی 'a roar/clamor' is the object or event, and برآورد (here the past of برآوردن) means 'raised/uttered' — the agent رخش follows as subject. The verb is past and the order is emphatic/poetic.
segments.s430-g70: بخندید is simple past 'he laughed'. شادان دل is an adjectival phrase where شادان 'joyful' modifies دل 'heart' (i.e., 'with a joyful heart'). تاج‌بخش is a title/name 'crown-giver' (the king) and functions as the subject for this verb; word order places predicate before some modifiers in the poetic line.
segments.s430-g71: بدو گفت is dative + past verb: بدو 'to him/to it' (dative) + گفت 'said' — together 'he said to it'. مرغ‌ای is a vocative 'O bird' (مرغ + vocative -ای). گو here is an imperative 'say' or 'tell' (short form of گفتن used as command). پیلتن is a proper name (or place-name) and in this compact imperative string functions as the indirect object 'to Piltan' (the recipient of the speech).
segments.s430-g72: توی is a locative preposition/adverb 'in/among'. نامبردار is an adjective 'renowned/famed' (literally 'named-notable') modifying انجمن 'assembly/gathering'; هر 'every' quantifies the noun phrase. The phrase forms the adverbial complement for the imperative 'speak/tell' — 'tell Piltan in every famed assembly.'
segments.s430-g105: بدین گیتیش is a prepositional phrase meaning “in this world.” بدین is an archaising fused preposition/demonstrative (ب- + دین) functioning like modern به/این to mean “in/with this,” and گیتیش is گیت(ی) “world” with the enclitic -ش; شوربختی is the noun “misfortune,” and بود is the past copula “was,” so the whole clause asserts existence: “there was misfortune in this world.”
segments.s430-g106: وگر introduces a conditional concessive sense “and/if (even)”; بگذرد is the 3rd-person present/subjunctive (ب‑ + گذرد) meaning “(if) it passes” or “should pass”; رنج و سختی is a coordinated noun phrase “suffering and hardship”; and بود (past copula) states existence in the past sense “there was.” Together the clause means roughly “and even if suffering passes, (there) was hardship.”
segments.s430-g107: شگفتی (a wonder/marvel) is the direct object, and نمایم is 1st-person present/subjunctive form of نمودن meaning “I show / I will show / I will cause.” هم امشب is an adverbial phrase “also tonight.” ترا is the object pronoun (ت + را) marking “you” as the definite object. The clause literally: “I will show a marvel even tonight to you.”
segments.s430-g108: ببندم is 1st-person present/subjunctive (ب‑ + بندم) here used as a future/intentional ‘I will bind/close/silence.’ ز is the preposition “because of / from,” گفتار بد is the noun phrase “bad/evil speech,” and لب ترا (لب + ت + را) is the object “your lips” (with the enclitic pronoun and object marker). The sense is idiomatic: “I will bind/silence you because of your ill speech / bad words.”
segments.s430-g109: برو is the imperative “go.” رخش رخشنده is an appositive noun phrase: رخش (the horse’s name) followed by the epithet رخشنده “radiant.” را is the definite object marker attached to the horse phrase, and برنشین is an imperative ‘mount / sit upon.’ The sequence is two imperatives: “Go; mount Rakhsh the radiant.”
segments.s430-g110: یکی here functions as an indefinite numeral “one / a certain” (i.e., ‘a’), خنجر is the noun “dagger,” آبگون is the adjective “bluish” (postpositive adjective following the noun in this style), and برگزین is the imperative “choose/select.” The phrase means “Choose a bluish dagger.”
segments.s430-g111: چو is the temporal conjunction “when”; بشنید is the past 3rd-person singular “he heard”; رستم is the subject name Rostam. میان را ببست is a verbal phrase where میان “the middle / the distance / the gap” is the object (را marks it) and ببست is past “he closed / he bound.” In context this idiom means he closed up the distance (moved in).
segments.s430-g112: وزان is a fused connective (و + زان) meaning “and from that / from that spot”; جایگه is “place / spot”; رخش را is the object-marked name of the horse; and برنشست is past “he sat upon / he mounted.” The phrase orders the place adverbial before the verb: “and from that place he mounted Rakhsh.”
segments.s430-g57: ازو (classical contracted from از او) is a prepositional phrase meaning 'from it/from him' placed preverbally. چار پیکان is a noun phrase 'four arrows' (چار = 'four'), and به بیرون کشید is a past-tense verb phrase where کشید is the past of کشیدن 'to pull' and به بیرون (towards/outside) is an adverbial phrase marking direction; the whole segment reads literally 'from it four arrows he pulled out'.
segments.s430-g58: به منقار is an instrumental phrase 'with/by the beak' (به used in the sense 'by/using'); از ان is a genitive/partitive 'from that', خستگی is the noun 'fatigue/weariness' (here referring to the afflicted spot), and خون کشید is a past active clause 'drew blood' (کشید = 'drew/pulled' in context). Word order places the instrument before the source and the verb at the end in classic style.
segments.s430-g59: بران is a locative/adverbial particle 'upon/onto them' (referring back to the previously mentioned things). خستگیها is plural 'the fatigues/wearinesses' and بمالید is a past-form of مالیدن 'to rub/anoint' (third person singular here); پر (feather) is the direct object placed after the verb, a typical flexible word order in classical Persian.
segments.s430-g60: هم اندر زمان is an adverbial phrase 'at the same moment' (هم = 'also/at once', اندر زمان = 'in the time/moment'); گشت is the past of گشتن meaning 'became/turned into'. با introduces the accompanying state 'with' and links زیب و فر ('beauty and royal splendour') as the resultant qualities.
segments.s430-g61: بدو is a dative/enclitic phrase 'to it/to him' (to the thing being addressed). گفت here functions as 'said to' (classical ordering: indirect object before گفت), کاین is the relative/demonstrative 'that these', خستگیها is the plural noun 'fatigues', and ببند is an imperative 'bind/close up' addressing the addressee; the clause is reported speech introduced by گفت.
segments.s430-g62: همی باش is a two-word imperative phrase: همی is an emphatic/adverbial particle meaning 'remain/still' and باش is the 2nd-person imperative 'be/stay'. یکچند دور از گزند is an adverbial adjunct 'for a while, away from harm' (یکچند = 'for a little while', دور از گزند = 'far from harm'), completing the content of the command.
segments.s430-g63: یکی is the numeral/determiner 'one' introducing a single item, پر is 'feather' as the noun, and من‌تر is an archaic/poetic modifier attached to پر (its exact micro-meaning is archaic and context-dependent); بگردان is an imperative 'turn!' directing the action and به شیر is the prepositional phrase 'toward the lion' indicating the target direction.
segments.s430-g64: بمال is an imperative 'rub!/anoint!' (the verb مالیدن). اندران is a locative 'among/in them' or 'into it', and خستگیهای تیر is a genitive construction 'the fatigues/wearinesses of the arrows' (تیر modifying خستگیها). Together the phrase commands to anoint/ rub that feather with the arrow-caused fatigues.
segments.s430-g89: نجویی is a verbal-noun meaning 'pursuit/quest' and here takes فزونی (an attributive noun 'increase/more') after it: 'an increase of pursuit'. The phrase is directed with به + proper name (به اسفندیار) = 'toward Esfandiyar.' Word order is poetic (noun + modifier + به + object).
segments.s430-g90: گه is an adverb 'at times/sometimes.' کوشش and جستن are gerundial/noun-like infinitives here ('striving' and 'seeking') joined by و 'and'; کارزار is their object ('battle'). The clause is nominal/elliptical (no finite verb) typical of poetic listing.
segments.s430-g91: ور is the conditional 'if.' ایدونک is a temporal particle 'now/at this moment.' او را uses the object/topic marker را attached after the pronoun to indicate 'to him' (classical word order); بیامد is 3rd-person past 'came' used in the sense 'befall/come upon,' and زمان is 'time' — together: 'If now a time comes upon him.'
segments.s430-g92: نیندیشی is a negative imperative/prohibitive addressed to 'you' (classical 2sg form) meaning 'do not think/suppose.' از is the preposition 'about/of/regarding,' پوزش is 'pardon/excuse,' and بی‌گمان is an adverb 'undoubtedly' (here reinforcing the prohibition: 'do not count on pardon, surely').
segments.s430-g93: پس‌انگه is a temporal/transition adverb 'then/afterwards.' یکی چاره is an indefinite noun phrase 'a certain remedy/one expedient.' سازم is 1st-person singular present/future 'I will make/devise'; ترا is the 2nd-person object (ت) with the object marker را, 'for you.' The clause literally: 'Then I will make a certain remedy for you.'
segments.s430-g94: به introduces the goal/standard 'to/toward.' خورشید is literal/metaphorical 'the sun' (here a lofty standard of glory), سر 'head' is the thing being exalted, and برفرازم is a 1st‑person verb form 'I raise/exalt (upwards)' (poetic morphology). ترا (again) marks the person affected. The whole: 'I will raise your head up to the sun.'
segments.s430-g95: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when.' بشنید is 3rd-person past 'he heard,' with the subject name رستم preceding it. دلش is 'his heart' (دل + possessive suffix ش), and شاد شد is the compound meaning 'became glad' (شاد 'happy' + شد 'became').
segments.s430-g96: از is the preposition 'from/out of.' اندیشه is 'thought/intention,' and بستن here is a verbal noun/infinitive 'to bind/to restrain' (i.e. the idea of restraint or binding). آزاد is the adjective 'free,' and شد is the past auxiliary 'became' — together: 'he became free from the thought of binding/restraint.'
segments.s430-g145: بدو here is an old dative/prepositional phrase 'to him' placed before the verb for emphasis; گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (third-person) reporting speech. اکنون is a temporal adverb 'now' that scopes the subordinate clause introduced by چو 'when', which here begins the clause whose verb appears in the next segment.
segments.s430-g146: بیاید and بجوید are third-person present/subjunctive forms used to express a future event in a temporal 'when' clause: 'when he comes, (he) will seek/demand.' ز is the poetic preposition 'from' marking the source/target of the action, and تو is the second-person pronoun clitic meaning 'you.' کارزار is the object 'battle/struggle' and completes the phrase 'seek battle from you.' Word order is typical classical Persian with the subordinate verb cluster carrying the tense/mood.
segments.s430-g147: تو is the explicit subject 'you' (used for emphasis). خواهش کن is an imperative construction: خواهش (a request) plus the imperative verb کن 'do/make' — 'make a request/plead.' The following nouns لابه and راستی are coordinated by و and share the imperative sense (i.e., 'entreat and be honest'), with راستی functioning adverbially as 'sincerely/honestly.' The pronoun and imperatives create an exhortation.
segments.s430-g148: مکوب here is best read as a passive/predicative form 'be struck / be crushed' (i.e., do not let yourself be struck); ایچ گونه = 'in no such manner / thus' (ایچ = 'not at all', گونه = 'manner, way'), and در کاستی = 'by lack/poverty' (در = 'in/by', کاستی = 'deficiency'). Together they form a prohibitive wish: 'do not be struck down in this way by want.'
segments.s430-g149: مگر introduces an exception/conditional 'unless' here. بازگردد is a jussive/optative third-person form 'may return / returns again.' The prepositional phrase به شیرین سخن ('to sweet speech') means 'to pleasant/sweet talk' and functions adverbially describing the manner or content of his returning (i.e., returning to speaking kindly).
segments.s430-g150: بیاد آیدش is an idiomatic sequence meaning 'comes to his memory' or 'he remembers' — بیاد is an adverbial 'to memory' and آید with the clitic -ش 'to him' yields 'it comes to him.' روزگار کهن (postposed) is the theme 'the days of old' that comes to mind; the word order places emphasis on 'روزگار کهن.'
segments.s430-g151: که introduces an explanatory relative/that-clause referring back to the preceding statement. تو is the subject 'you.' چند گه is a temporal expression 'for some time / at several times / sometimes' (چند = some/several, گه = occasion/sometimes). بودی is the past-tense second-person 'you were.' اندر جهان is the locative phrase 'in the world / in this life.'
segments.s430-g152: به رنج و به سختی repeats the preposition به ('with / in') for parallelism: 'with toil and with hardship.' ز بهر is a frozen poetic prepositional phrase meaning 'for the sake of / on account of,' and مهان is the purpose/beneficiary 'guests.' The whole phrase states the reason for the suffering: 'for the sake of guests.'
segments.s430-g137: 'بر آتش' places the action spatially: 'on/over the fire.' 'مرین' here is a vocative/proper name (addressing someone called Marin) rather than a verb. 'چوب را' is the definite object marked by 'را' (the shaft/wood), and 'راست کن' is an imperative phrase 'make straight / straighten (it).' Word order is poetic: prepositional phrase + vocative + object + verb(imperative).
segments.s430-g138: 'نگه کن' is an imperative composed of the noun/command 'نگه' (look/keep/watch) plus the auxiliary imperative 'کن' = 'do/keep.' 'یکی' is an indefinite numeral 'one/a,' modifying 'پیکان.' 'نغز' is an evaluative adjective 'fine/delicate/delightful.' 'کهن' qualifies the arrow as 'old/ancient.' The whole clause is a short imperative: 'Watch/attend — (make) one fine old arrow.'
segments.s430-g139: This clause strings imperative instructions. 'بنه' functions here as an imperative 'bind/attach / fit' (an archaic/poetic imperative form used to tell someone to fasten things). 'پر و پیکان' are the objects 'feathers and the shaft/arrow.' The repeated 'و' links items and then the verb 'برو' ('go') gives the next command. 'بر نشان' is a prepositional phrase 'to/on the mark/target.'
segments.s430-g140: 'نمودم' is the first-person past 'I showed' (classical past). The following 'ترا' written as the clitic split into 'ت' + 'را' marks the second-person object 'you' with the object marker. 'از گزندش' is a protective/genitive phrase 'from its harm' ('از' = from, 'گزند' = harm, + possessive '-ش' = 'its'). The final 'نشان' is the thing shown: 'a sign/mark' — i.e., 'I showed you a mark to guard you from its harm.' Word order is typical older Persian: verb + clitic object + modifiers + object noun at end.
segments.s430-g141: 'چو' here means 'when.' 'ببرید' is a past action 'cut (off)' (poetic past form placed before the subject). 'رستم' is the subject following the verb (inverted poetic order). 'تن' = 'body/trunk' and 'شاخ گز' = 'the branch of the tamarisk' (i.e., 'the trunk of the tamarisk branch'). Together they mean 'when Rostam severed the trunk/shaft of the tamarisk branch.'
segments.s430-g142: 'بیامد' is simple past 'he came.' 'ز' is the poetic form of 'از' = 'from,' so 'ز دریا' = 'from the sea.' 'به ایوان' = 'to the iwan/portico.' The final 'و رز' joins with 'بیامد' as a brief comment on his appearance: 'and (he came) rosy' — 'رز' used as 'rosy hue' describing him.
segments.s430-g143: بران کار is a prepositional/locative phrase meaning 'on/with respect to that affair' placed before the rest of the clause for emphasis. سیمرغ is the grammatical subject; بد رهنمای is a predicative noun phrase ('bad guide') with the adjective بد before the noun رهنمای. There is no copula here because the verb 'بود' appears in the next segment and links back to this predicate.
segments.s430-g144: همی بود is a literary (classical) past-continuous construction: همی adds iterative/continuous aspect and بود is the past copula 'was'. بر تارک او به پای is a locative phrase: بر + [تارک او] 'upon his (tark)' followed by به پای 'at/near the foot' (here 'تارک' functions as a top-of-head/crown location and او = 'him'). Word order places the continuous verb before the locative phrase it governs, typical of Ferdowsi's style.
segments.s430-g49: گر is the conditional 'if'. ایدونک is an archaic temporal particle meaning 'now' or 'at this moment'. رستم is the subject. نگردد is the negative present/subjunctive form of گشتن/گردیدن ('become, come to be'); with the negative prefix ن- it means 'does not become/return (sound)'. درست here functions as a predicate adjective meaning 'sound/unharmed' (an older usage not listed in the simple dictionary senses). Word order is poetic: conditional particle + time + subject + verb + predicate.
segments.s430-g50: کجا 'where' introduces the question. خواهم is the 1st‑person auxiliary 'I will / shall' used here to ask 'where shall I...'. اندر 'in / within' and جهان 'the world' form the locative phrase 'in the world'. جای is 'place, refuge'. جست is the classical verb-form of جستن 'to seek' used after the auxiliary (classical ordering places the auxiliary before the main verb); together 'خواهم ... جست' = 'shall I seek'.
segments.s430-g51: همه سیستان = 'all (of) Sistan' (object phrase placed before the verb as a topicalized object). پاک here is adverbial 'completely/utterly'. ویران کنند is a 3rd‑person plural present/subjunctive used narratively to express future/intent: 'they make (it) desolate / they will lay waste'. The verb is کن(دن) with ویران as the complement meaning 'to ruin/desolate'.
segments.s430-g52: به کام = 'to the desire/for the satisfaction of' (a prepositional phrase). دلیران ایران 'the brave ones of Iran' is the genitive group modified by به کام. کنند repeats the plural verbal form, linking this clause to the preceding one: 'they do/bring about (it) to the liking of Iran's warriors.'
segments.s430-g53: شود is the 3rd‑person present/subjunctive or potential of شدن 'become / may be', used here to present a possible outcome: 'should be / be (it)'. کنده is the past participle 'uprooted/torn up'. این تخمه ما = 'this seed/stock of ours' (تخمه here = 'offspring, stock'). ز بن 'from the root' is a poetic prepositional phrase. The whole segment expresses a feared result: 'this offspring of ours may be uprooted from the root.'
segments.s430-g54: کنون 'now' sets the time. بر چه is an older phrasing meaning 'for what reason / why'. رانیم is 1st‑person plural present/subjunctive of راندن 'to drive' (here idiomatically 'to utter / to pursue' as in سخن راندن = to speak/continue speaking); the rhetoric is 'why should we (still) do X'. یکسر 'utterly/at all' modifies سخن 'speech / speaking'. Syntactically this is a rhetorical question: 'if our line is uprooted, why should we now at all speak?'.
segments.s430-g55: نگه کرد is a compound of the noun نگه 'gaze' + the light verb کرد 'did' = 'it looked/gazed'. مرغ is the subject 'the bird'. اندران (اندر + آن) = 'in/within it / among them' (poetic locative). خستگی 'weariness, fatigue' is the thing noticed. Word order is inverted for poetic effect: verb + subject + locative + object.
segments.s430-g56: بدید is the past tense 'saw'. اندرو = 'in it / therein' (synonymous/parallel to اندران). راه 'way, path' and پیوستگی 'joining/connectedness' form a noun phrase 'the way of joining' or 'the path to reunion' which the bird perceived. Together: 'it saw therein the path of joining.'
segments.s430-g81: که begins a relative/explanatory clause 'that/for' linking to the previous sentence. اندر زمانه is a prepositional phrase 'in that time/age' (اندر = in, زمانه = age). چنویی is an adverbial adjective 'such/like that', and نخاست is a past intransitive verb 'did not arise/appear' (archaic classical usage). Together the segment says 'that in the age no one of that sort arose.'
segments.s430-g82: بدو دارد is literal 'has/holds for him' where بدو = 'to him/unto it' and دارد is the present indicative of داشتن 'to have, to give' used here as 'would give/offer'. ایران is the agent/nation 'Iran', همی is an archaic intensifier/adverb (here 'indeed/so'), and پشت راست is a compound meaning 'firm/upright back' used idiomatically for 'support/protection' (پشت = support, راست = straight/firm).
segments.s430-g83: بپرهیزی is a verbal/noun-form meaning 'avoidance / to abstain' (here rendered as 'you avoid' or 'to keep away'), followed by از وی 'from him'. نباشد شگفت is an impersonal/clausal phrase 'it is not a wonder' (نباشد = 'is not' in classical subjunctive/optative form, شگفت = 'wonder'). The clause therefore says 'it is no wonder if you avoid him.'
segments.s430-g84: مرا از خود اندازه باید گرفت is an idiomatic statement: مرا (me, here split in the listing as 'م' + 'را') is the definite object 'me', از خود 'by/from myself', اندازه 'measure/standard', باید گرفت is a semi-impersonal necessity 'one must take' (باید = must, گرفت = take). So: 'one must take measure of me by myself' → 'one must judge me by my own measure.' Note: the word list splits مرا into 'م' and 'را'; 'م' in the listing does not reflect the usual pronominal sense directly and is treated as part of the object 'مرا'.
segments.s430-g85: که marks a relative clause 'that/which'. آن جفت من مرغ = 'that pair of my birds' (آن = that, جفت = pair, من = my/I used genitively here, مرغ = bird). با دستگاه immediately following qualifies how they were handled: با + دستگاه = 'with a device/method/force' (classical poetic 'with a contrivance' or 'by force/means').
segments.s430-g86: به دستان و شمشیر کردش تباه is a verbal clause 'he made them ruin/ruined them': به دستان و شمشیر = 'by (his) hands and sword' (به = by/with, دستان = hands, و = and, شمشیر = sword). کردش is the transitive past 'he did/made it (them)' with the object-clitic -ش referring back to 'جفت', and تباه is the adjective 'ruined/destroyed'. Word order places instrument before the verb in this classical style.
segments.s430-g87: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. با من اکنون تو پیمان کنی is the protasis: با من = 'with me', اکنون = 'now', تو = 'you' (emphatic), پیمان = 'pact/pledge', and کنی is the present/subjunctive 2nd-person singular of کردن used for 'you make/enter into'. Word order is typical classical Persian (adverb within the clause).
segments.s430-g88: سر از جنگ جستن پشمان کنی is the apodosis: سر 'head' used idiomatically with جستن 'to seek' to mean 'to take up/attempt' (سر جستن = to set out), از جنگ = 'from/for war' (i.e. 'to wage war'), پشمان = 'repentant/sorry', and کنی is again 2nd-person present/subjunctive 'you will be/make yourself'. The whole clause = 'you will be sorry for taking up war' → 'you will regret engaging in war.'
segments.s430-g153: This is a conditional clause introduced by چو + subjunctive: چو + پوزش + کنی = 'if/when you make an apology'. چند is used adverbially here = 'some / a few' modifying نپذیردت. نپذیردت is a negative present/subjunctive form with the clitic -ت 'you' as object: نپذیرد + ت = 'they will not accept you / some will not accept you.' Word order is topic (چو پوزش کنی چند) + predicate (نپذیردت).
segments.s430-g154: همی is an adverb here meaning 'still/always' or 'even so'. از marks the agent/source ('by/from') and فرومایگان is the agent noun 'the lowly / wretches'. گیردت is a future/present-stative verb form with the clitic -ت 'you' = '(they) will take/catch you.' The clause means 'and yet the lowly will seize you.'
segments.s430-g155: This is an imperative: به + زه + کن + کمان را = 'attach / string the bow with the bowstring'. Here به functions as a prepositional/benefactive marker ('with / by means of'), زه = 'bowstring', کن = imperative 'do / make', and کمان را is the definite object marked by را. The second clause begins with و این چوب گز = 'and this tamarisk wood' (گز = tamarisk).
segments.s430-g156: بدین گونه is a frozen phrase meaning 'in this manner / thus' (here written as ب + دین + گونه in the segmentation). پرورده is a participle/adjective 'raised / cultivated / seasoned' modifying چوب; در آب رز is a locative phrase 'in rose-water' specifying how it was treated. The sequence places the manner phrase before the participle and locative: 'this wood, cultivated in this way in rose-water.'
segments.s430-g157: This is an imperative construction with the verb کن 'do' acting on preceding nominal phrases. ابر چشم او is a genitive phrase 'the cloud of his eye' (a poetic image for eyelid/eye's veil), راست کن = 'make straight', and the following هر دو دست ('both hands') is coordinated as a second object/phrase also to be made straight (elliptical imperatival repetition). Word order: object(s) before the imperative verb, a common poetic/orderly inversion.
segments.s430-g158: چنانچون introduces a simile 'just as / like', followed by بود (past 'was') describing مردم گزپرست 'the gaz‑worshipping people'. گزپرست is a compound noun (گز + پرست 'worshipper/devotee') used adjectivally to characterize the folk. The clause reads 'just as the gaz‑worshipping people were.'
segments.s430-g159: زمانه is the subject 'time / fortune'. برد here functions as a verbal past/predicate 'took/put/carried' (poetic usage), followed by راست 'straight/plain' describing manner and آن را 'that (thing)' as the object, and به چشم 'before the eye / to the sight' as a prepositional phrase. Word order is subject – verb – object – adverbial ('time put that straight before the eye').
segments.s430-g160: بدانگه 'thereupon / at that moment' introduces a temporal clause; که functions as the subordinating conjunction 'when/that', followed by باشد (subjunctive/present of 'to be') and دلت (دل + ت = 'your heart') as the subject of the clause. پر ز خشم is a predicate phrase 'full of anger' (پر = 'full (of)', ز = 'of/from', خشم = 'anger'). The clause means 'at that moment when your heart is full of anger.'
segments.s430-g129: بمالید is a past tense verb ('he rubbed' / 'he applied'); here it carries the literal sense of rubbing or smearing. The prepositional phrase بر تارکش ('on/against its tar') follows the verb; تارکش is a possessive form (tar + -ش) referring to the instrument/string belonging to someone, and پر خویش is 'his own feather' (پر = feather, خویش formed from خوی + ش = 'one's own'). Word order is verb → locative → object phrase, a common poetic ordering.
segments.s430-g130: بفرمود is an honorific/past verb here meaning 'he said/commanded.' تا introduces a purposive clause ('so that / in order that'), and the clause رستم آمدش پیش is the result: 'Rostam came to him / came before him' (آمدش = 'came to him', پیش = 'in the presence of'). The whole clause reports an order that produced Rostam's approach.
segments.s430-g131: گزی is the past action from گزیدن used here poetically ('he chose/selected'); دید is the simple past 'he saw' or 'he perceived'—together they convey the action of selecting a perch. The following locative phrases بر خاک ('on the earth') and سر بر هوا ('head toward the air') describe posture and place; Persian poetry freely places these phrases after the verb for vivid effect.
segments.s430-g132: نشست is the past of نشستن ('sat' / 'perched'). The prepositional/source marker از combines with برش to indicate the thing on which the action occurred (here, 'on it' / 'upon that'), and مرغ فرمانروا is the subject in post-verbal position ('the sovereign bird'). This verb‑subject order is typical in epic poetry: 'the sovereign bird sat upon it.'
segments.s430-g133: بدو گفت is 'he said to him' (بدو = 'to him/unto it'); گفت here functions as the reporting verb. شاخی گزین is an imperative instruction: شاخی ('a branch' / 'branchy') + گزین (imperative 'choose/select'). راست‌تر is the comparative 'straighter' or 'more straight' modifying شاخی. The phrase is a direct command placed after the reporting phrase.
segments.s430-g134: سرش is 'its head' (سر + possessive ش); برترین is a superlative/adjectival form ('most elevated' / 'foremost') describing the head. و links the two qualities; تنش is 'its body/trunk' (تن + ش) and کاست‌تر is comparative 'more reduced' / 'slimmer'. The construction gives two coordinated comparative qualities to prefer in the branch.
segments.s430-g135: بدان گز بود هوش اسفندیار is a compact, slightly archaic phrasing: بدان گز ('in that choice' / 'to that selection') + بود ('was') + هوش اسفندیار ('the wit/wisdom of Esfandiyar'). Grammatically the sentence attributes the soundness of the chosen bough to Esfandiyar's intelligence.
segments.s430-g136: تو این چوب را خوار مایه مدار is an admonition addressed to 'you' (تو). این چوب را uses the object marker را to mark 'this stick'; خوار مایه is a nominal phrase meaning 'a thing of contempt / a worthless matter'; مدار here functions idiomatically as a negative directive ('do not make/regard it as ...' — a poetic imperative). The overall sense is 'Do not treat/consider this wood as worthless.'
segments.s430-g113: به + noun + گفت is a prepositional phrase + verb: به سیمرغ گفت = 'he said to the Simurgh.' The vocative follows the reporting verb (ای گزین جهان = 'O, chooser of the world'), where ای marks direct address and گزین (literally 'chooser') is used as a title with جهان as its complement.
segments.s430-g114: This is an interrogative clause. چه = 'what', خواهد is the present/future auxiliary meaning 'will' or 'will happen', بر + ین (orthographic variant of این) = 'upon this', مرگ ما = 'our death', and ناگهان 'suddenly' modifies the event. Word order places the auxiliary before the locative phrase (literal: 'what will upon-this our death suddenly?').
segments.s430-g115: جهان is the subject ('the world'). یادگارست is a poetic contraction of یادگار است = 'is a remembrance/memorial.' و connects clauses; ما رفتنی uses رفتنی (an adjectival participle meaning 'about to go' / 'ephemeral') as the predicate describing 'we' (i.e. 'we are transient').
segments.s430-g116: به گیتی = locative 'in the world' (به + گیتی). نماند is the predicate meaning 'remains / is left' (here used as general truth: 'there remains'); به جز introduces the exception 'except' and مردمی ('people' / 'the folk') is the exception. The order places the locative before the verb, then the exception phrase after.
segments.s430-g117: به نام نکو‌گر is a prepositional phrase meaning roughly 'with a good name' or 'in a noble name.' بمیرم is the 1st-person subjunctive ('that I die' / 'that I should die') used after a judgmental predicate. رواست (روا + است contracted) is a fixed copular expression 'it is fitting/permissible.' The construction = 'It is fitting that I die with a good name.'
segments.s430-g118: مرا نام باید is a dative/fronted-object pattern: مرا = 'for me' (dative/object), نام باید = 'a name is necessary' (باید = modal 'must/is necessary'). که introduces the causal clause 'because/for that': تن مرگ راست = 'the body’s death is true/certain' (راست = 'true/certain'). Together: 'A name is necessary for me, because the body's death is certain.' Note: the tokenization in the provided word list splits ملاک forms (مرا) across items; grammatically treat 'مرا' as the dative pronoun.
segments.s430-g119: کجا شد is an idiomatic interrogative 'where did [they] go/where are [they now]' (شد here functions as 'went/has become') and is followed by the names فریدون و هوشنگ with شاه appositive: both are being called 'king(s).' Word order puts the question word + verb before the named subjects for emphasis.
segments.s430-g120: که introduces a relative clause modifying the kings: بودند = past tense 'they were' (i.e. 'who were'), با = 'with/possessing', then a list of possessions گنج و تخت و کلاه (treasure, throne, and hat/crown). The clause describes what they once had.
